title: APF Paper 1 — The Enforceability of Distinction: The Structural Skeleton of Quantum Mechanics from Finite Enforcement Capacity
description:
23 machine-verifiable theorems deriving the structural skeleton of quantum mechanics from a single axiom: finite enforcement capacity (A1). Zero external dependencies, zero free parameters, exact rational arithmetic throughout.

This repository is the Interactive Mathematical Appendix for Paper 1 of the Admissibility Physics Framework (APF). It contains the complete derivation chain from Axiom A1 through four structural lemmas to the quantum skeleton: Hilbert spaces, the Born rule, CPTP dynamics, tensor products, gauge symmetry, and von Neumann entropy as committed capacity.

Key features

  • All pre-Hilbert-space theorems (A1 through T1) use only finite sets and exact rational arithmetic — no matrices, no complex numbers, no linear algebra
  • Matrices first appear in T2 as the minimal representation of the non-commutative structure T1 proves from scalars (Frobenius' theorem forces ℂ as the ground field)
  • Every theorem traces to a named check function with a six-field docstring (manuscript section, dependencies, mathematical statement, verification method, physical meaning)
  • Total verification time: <1 second

Three verification paths

  1. Zero-install Colab notebook — Open in Google Colab — 25 annotated cells with inline LaTeX and executable code; all critical arithmetic visible, not hidden behind function calls
  2. Interactive derivation DAG — Open in browser — hover for theorem summaries, click for dependencies, animated verification
  3. Local execution — clone the GitHub repository and run python run_checks.py (Python 3.8+, no pip install)

Repository contents

  • apf/core.py — 23 check functions, one per theorem
  • apf/apf_utils.py — exact matrix arithmetic infrastructure (post-T2 only)
  • REVIEWERS_GUIDE.md — 9-section peer-review walkthrough addressing the scalar-to-matrix boundary, the Frobenius argument for ℂ, and six falsification surfaces
  • APF_Reviewer_Walkthrough.ipynb — Colab notebook for referees
  • docs/index.html — interactive derivation DAG

Accompanies the manuscript The Enforceability of Distinction submitted to Foundations of Physics.
